title: rhinanthus asperulus subsp. rohlenae
intro: Rhinanthus asperulus subsp. rohlenae is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family, characterized by its unique ability to parasitize the roots of other plants to obtain nutrients. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Rhinanthus asperulus subsp. rohlenae include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory symptoms. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
